module-0-welcome-board-project.label: Module Project: Build a Welcome Board
module-0-welcome-board-project.summary: A club leader needs one welcome board that grows a little more polished with each step. Keep improving the same program until the full board is ready.

sketch0.title: Your first client: a club leader with an empty welcome board

mp-1-print-event-title.title: Set the event title
mp-1-print-event-title.prompt: Start the real welcome board. Replace the preview line with one line that prints exactly `Code Club Open House`.
mp-1-print-event-title.hint: You only need one print line in this step.

help.concept: The project now shifts from the preview into the real board. Keep it simple and get the heading correct first.
help.hint_1: Remove the preview text and print the event title instead.
help.hint_2: Match the capitalization and spacing exactly.

mp-1-print-event-title.starterCode:
print("Welcome to Python Club")

# Replace the preview line with the real event title.

mp-2-print-agenda-lines.title: Add the time line
mp-2-print-agenda-lines.prompt: Keep the title line from the previous step. Under it, add one new line that prints exactly `Doors open at 4 PM`.
mp-2-print-agenda-lines.hint: Leave the title in place and add one more print line below it.

help.concept: This step extends the same program instead of starting over. Keep the working line you already have, then add the next detail.
help.hint_1: The output now has two lines.
help.hint_2: The title should still print first.

mp-2-print-agenda-lines.starterCode:
print("Code Club Open House")

# Keep the title line.
# Add the time line under it.

mp-3-show-seat-count.title: Add the helper count
mp-3-show-seat-count.prompt: Keep the two board lines you already have. Add a third line that prints exactly `Helpers ready: 6`, and calculate the `6` with `2 * 3` instead of typing it directly.
mp-3-show-seat-count.hint: Add one more print line and let Python evaluate `2 * 3`.

help.concept: Now the board mixes text with a small expression. That keeps the program readable while still letting Python do the number work.
help.hint_1: Keep the first two lines exactly as they are.
help.hint_2: The new line should print the label first and the calculated value second.

mp-3-show-seat-count.starterCode:
print("Code Club Open House")
print("Doors open at 4 PM")

# Keep the lines above.
# Add the helper count with the expression 2 * 3.

mp-4-build-full-welcome-board.title: Finish the welcome board
mp-4-build-full-welcome-board.prompt: Keep the board you have built so far. Add one last line under it that prints exactly `Bring your questions!`.
mp-4-build-full-welcome-board.hint: Do not rewrite the earlier lines. Just add the final line at the bottom.

help.concept: The last step is a small polish step, not a reset. Keep the working board and add the final message so the whole program feels finished.
help.hint_1: The final output has four lines.
help.hint_2: The new line belongs after the helper count.

mp-4-build-full-welcome-board.starterCode:
print("Code Club Open House")
print("Doors open at 4 PM")
print("Helpers ready:", 2 * 3)

# Keep the board above.
# Add one last line that says Bring your questions!

try-module-0-welcome-board-project-sketc.title: Preview the welcome board
try-module-0-welcome-board-project-sketc.prompt: Start the board with one friendly line: print exactly `Welcome to Python Club`.

help.concept: This quick preview shows the kind of clean output the project will build.
help.hint_1: You only need one line of text for this preview.

try_module_0_welcome_board_project_sketc.title: Try it yourself
try_module_0_welcome_board_project_sketc.prompt: Start the board with one friendly line: print exactly `Welcome to Python Club`.

try-module-0-welcome-board-project-sketc.prompt: Start the board with one friendly line: print exactly `Welcome to Python Club`.

help.concept: This quick preview shows the kind of clean output the project will build.
help.hint_1: You only need one line of text for this preview.

sketch-1.title: Your first client: a club leader with an empty welcome board
sketch-1.bodyMarkdown:
A club leader is setting up the first Python meetup and wants one simple welcome board on the screen before students walk in. The board does not need fancy features yet. It just needs to grow cleanly, one useful line at a time.

This module project is meant to feel like one small build. Each step starts with the program from the previous step, then adds one clear improvement so the finished board grows naturally.
